learning_outcomes.description: Construct and interpret a cost benefit analysis in a variety of real world situations.
Apply non-market valuation methods to correct market failures, including those of a social and/or intangible nature.
Evaluate risk and uncertainty in decision-making within a cost benefit analysis framework.
Critically evaluate the ethical and social consequences of decision-making using cost benefit analysis.

assessments.task_description:
Submissions not using the provided template will receive a zero.

Students will be required to submit solutions to computer-based exercises, case studies and short-answer questions as specified in Blackboard. Weekly tutorial sessions begin in week 2. Submission dates for tutorial exercises are indicated on the Blackboard website.

There will be 10 weekly tutorial session submissions for which you are expected to prepare and submit a set of fairly straight-forward Excel-based exercises in advance of each week's session. In each session a tutor will work through that week's exercise 'live' and, if time permits, cover a few other topics of relevance. 

Students will receive up to 10% of the final mark through 'active participation' in these tutorial sessions. 'Active participation' requires students to submit their Excel exercises or word document via the link on the course website. Blank submissions do not receive marks.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students can submit their attempt at the tutorial exercises prior or during their enrolled tutorial.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submissions after the deadline for the student's allocated tutorial will not be accepted. A penalty of 100% for non-submission will be applied.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
In Tutorial Week 4

In Tutorial Week 8

In Tutorial Week 13
assessments.task_description:
Students are required to complete three (3) quizzes covering both numerical and theoretical applications of CBA at regular intervals throughout the course. There will be three quizzes worth 10% each (total of 30% for the course).

Student IDs are mandatory. Students must present a valid university-issued ID card to be admitted into the room for the quiz. Without it, entry will not be permitted. Entry to the session is only provided to students on the enrolled tutorial list.

  • Students will have 30 minutes to complete each quiz.
  • There will be true/false, multiple choice, and numerical questions in this quiz. 
  • Students must attend their allocated tutorial in Week 4, Week 8, and Week 13 to complete the quiz.
  • Students should be at the venue for your quiz on time in the weeks of assessment. You must attend your allocated tutorial.
  • If you arrive late to the session, you will not receive additional time to complete the quiz.
  • Only UQ approved calculators are allowed.
  • More details regarding the format of the quiz will be posted to Blackboard under Assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 100% will be applied for non-submission unless an extension has been granted.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
One short assignment worth a total of 20%. Students are required to answer several short questions, both computational and answers to short questions, based on the previous weeks' lectures. Marks per question will be indicated.

The assignment must be submitted electronically through the relevant Assessment section of the Course Blackboard site. All working must be provided in Excel and submitted via the Online Submission link. Formatting requirements of the Excel spreadsheet will be provided. Formatting of student submissions must be consistent with the in-class materials provided. Students must submit their "Test" with their short responses and their Excel Spreadsheet with the fully worked answers and responses to text-based questions.

Expected time allocation is 7.5 - 10 hours to complete this assessment.

The assignment will be due in week 11.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submitted via both the test link and Excel submission portal in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Submissions not using the provided template will receive a zero.

Students are required to complete a take-home group case study assignment. Students will have two weeks to complete the assignment. The assignment will be released no later than 26th October. 

The assignment will consist of a group task worth 30% (spreadsheet and report) and an individual reflection worth 10% for a total of 40% of your final grade. The case study can be done individually or as a group of TWO students. Please note that this task is a significant amount of work for an individual, so groups are encouraged. Expected number of hours for this assessment is 15 - 25 hours of work. Students are expected to form their own groups using the sign-up sheet on Blackboard. If you do not sign up via the tool you are not in a group and must complete the assignment individually. A deadline for the creation of groups will be posted on Blackboard. No changes to groups can be made after this deadline.

The assignment must be submitted electronically through the Online Submission link in the Assessment section of the Course Blackboard site. Students are responsible for ensuring one student from the group submits the report and spreadsheet. Reflections must be submitted individually.

Further details to be announced in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
